name: Road Centerlines
description: Roads represents center lines for all streets, major roads and highways in Clark County.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Roads.gdb\Roads

name: Railroads
description: Railroads represent rail lines in Clark County and surrounding counties.See Clark County Metadata

name: C-TRAN Bus Routes
description: C-Tran bus routes information. C-TRAN serves the Vancouver Urban Growth Area in addition to the city limits of Camas, Washougal, Ridgefield, La Center, Battle Ground, and Yacolt. Reproduced with permission granted by C-TRAN. The information has been provided by means of a nonexclusive, limited, and revocable license granted by C-TRAN. C-TRAN does not guarantee the accuracy, adequacy, completeness or usefulness of any information. C-TRAN provides this information "as is," without warran

name: Bike Routes
description: Bike Routes shows bike paths, bike lanes and shared roadways in Clark County.See Clark County MetadataData source: ..\FGDB\dw_Transportation.gdb\BikeRoutes

name: School Facilities
description: School owned property. K-12 and accredited colleges and universities.Includes private schools.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\Schparc

name: Public Lands
description: Land that is owned by public entities.  Examples of public entity would be State of Washington, Clark County, US Forest Service, cities, Vancouver Housing Authority.See Clark County Metadata

name: Park Lands
description: Public parks, natural areas, and open spaces in Clark County. See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Parks.gdb\Parks

name: Zoning
description: Adopted zoning classifications.The zoning layer is a combination of the zoning layers for each city and unincorporated Clark County.  For more details about a specific zoning classification or boundary please contact the jurisdiction.See Clark County Metadata

name: Zoning Overlay
description: Zoning overlays show designated land uses in Clark County that are in addition to those in zoning. Examples of zoning overlays include urban reserve, urban holding, mining, Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA), and contingency zoning.This zoning overly layer reflects the county's Comprehensive Plan update of June 2016, plus any updates from Land Use Hearings Board rulings, court decisions, or municipalities. See the attached document for a listing of dates of when major updates wer

name: Urban Growth Area (UGA) Boundaries
description: Current urban growth area boundaries for Growth Management Act (GMA) planning.   This is UGA boundary layer used on most maps.  It is the entire UGA.  See CityUGA if  you need to differentiate between the City and City's UGA.This layer is similar to UGA.  The difference is that the  UGA layer identifies which areas were added during the last comprehensive plan process.   See Clark County Metadata

name: Comprehensive Plan
description: Current comprehensive plan designations for Clark County as adopted in the most recent Comprehensive Plan, including amendments. The comprehensive plan map is required by the State of Washington's Growth Management Act (GMA) which was first adopted in 1990.​See Clark County Metadata

name: Park Impact Fee Districts
description: Park Districts are used for managing park impact fees (PIFs) that are collected from residential construction in the Vancouver Urban Growth Area. Funds must be spent in the same park district that they were collected. The City of Vancouver manages park impact fees within the city and Clark County manages park impact fees in the unincorporated area.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Parks.gdb\parkdst

name: Taxlots for Public Use
description: Represents both land parcels and any tax accounts associated with them. In the case of condominiums and mobile homes, this can result in many taxlots being stacked on top of one another. This layer is used for property assessment and taxation purposes and is updated on a weekly basis. Please note that taxlot boundaries are not surveys and cannot be used to determine the location of property lines on the ground. This is a version of our original Taxlots layer that is intended for public use. All

name: Subdivisions
description: Recorded subdivisions, short plats, and commercial plats.   This layer includes all recorded plats.  This means there are overlapping boundaries.   See SubdivLatest for a non overlapping layer that shows only the most recently recorded plat.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_clark.gdb\Subdiv

name: Tax Code Districts
description: Tax Code Districts combines individual taxing districts (i.e., fire, cemetery, school) into a single entity to show uniform taxing areas in Clark County.See Clark County Metadata

name: School Districts
description: School districtsSee Clark County Metadata

name: Situs Addresses
description: Physical addresses in Clark County. This includes apartments and other multiple addresses for a single property.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\Situs

name: Historic Districts
description: Historic Districts are areas in Clark County that are recognized on national, state, or county historic registers.A Historic District possesses a significant concentration, linkage, or continuity of sites, buildings, structures, or objects united historically or aesthetically by plan or physical development. Districts include college campuses, central business districts, residential areas, commercial areas, large forts, industrial complexes, civic centers, rural villages, canal systems, collecti

name: Fire Districts
description: Fire Districts in Clark County.   It also includes the responding fire agency and district level demographic data for the current year.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\Firedst

name: Election Precincts
description: Most current Election Precinct district boundaries as received from the County Elections department. These areas are also known as Election Districts or Voting Districts.See Clark County Metadata

name: City Limits
description: Municipal jurisdiction boundaries including Clark County.See Clark County Metadata

name: Census Blocks - 2020

columns.description: American Indian and Alaska Native Population

columns.name: Native Hawaiian and Other Pacific Islander Population
columns.description: Native Hawaiian and Other Pacific Islander Population

columns.name: Population of Two or More Races
columns.description: Population of Two or More Races

columns.description: Non-Hispanic American Indian and Alaska Native Population

columns.description: Non-Hispanic Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander Population

columns.name: Non-Hispanic Two or More Races Population
columns.description: Non-Hispanic Two or More Races Population

description: The US Census Blocks used for the 2020 decennial census in Clark County are the smallest geographic units used to summarize statistics from the 100-percent count of population on April 1, 2020.  In Clark County,  blocks may have a population as high as 2300, but on average, populated census blocks have around 90 people.   (The median number is 49.)Attributes of census block areas report population by race, selected ethnic groups, and number of households.To protect privacy, the US Census has add

name: Trail
description: Trails shows the locations of established and conceptual trails in Clark County.  Where available, trail use (hiking, biking, equestrian, and motorized), surface type, distance, and other characteristics are listed in the attributes.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Parks.gdb\Trails

name: Clark County Surface Treatments
description: Historic surface treatment projects (chip seal, hot mix, slurry seat, etc.).See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Roads.gdb\SurfTrtmnts

name: C-TRAN Trapeze Roads
description: A combined dataset of roads, railroads and streams in and near Clark County modified to be used by C-Trans Trapeze routing software.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_Transportation.gdb\CTranTrapezeRoads

name: Unrecorded Surveys
description: Bounding area of Unrecorded Surveys as received and scanned by the County Surveyors office.See Clark County Metadata

name: Survey Monuments
description: Surveyed Points (Public Land Survey Section Corners, Donation Land Claim Corners, etc.)See Clark County Metadata

name: Recorded Surveys
description: Bounding area of Surveys as recorded with the County Auditors office. These are general outlines of the area surveyed, see recorded image for exact boundaries, dimensions, etc.See Clark County Metadata

name: Benchmark
description: Created from description field from Survey Control Database in Surveyor's Office. X and Y coordinates are for general reference only. Elevations are of survey level accuracy. Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\benchmrk

name: Vacant Buildable Lands Model
description: The Vacant Buildable Lands Model (VBLM) is an annual analysis of the additional housing and employment capacity within the current Urban Growth Areas (UGA) of Clark County. The VBLM is a core component of the Comprehensive Planning process and is required by Washington State's Growth Management Act.Clark County has been running the VBLM since 1994. This archive begins in 1996. The VBLM is the final product of the annual VBLM run. This version of the model is optimized for analysis and display of

name: Comprehensive Plan Overlay
description: Overlay to accompany the comprehensive plan (COMPLAN) layer.  Shows areas representing: Columbia River Gorge Scenic Area, industrial reserve, mining lands, and others.  Overlays may limit  or allow specific uses  in addition to the comprehensive plan designations.See Clark County Metadata

name: Townships
description: Public Land Survey Townships. Refers to a rectangular unit of land that is nominally six miles on a side.See Clark County Metadata

name: Quarter Section
description: Control Layer used as base for parcels layer. Contains: Polygons, lines and tics for quarter section. Quarter sections are numbered 1 to 4 counterclockwise beginning in the northeast quarter section.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_clark.gdb\qsection

name: Legislative Election Districts
description: Washington State Legislative Election Districts. See Clark County Metadata

name: Election Portions of Precincts
description: Election precincts have been subdivided into portions to account for slight differences in election ballots within a precinct. All precincts have at least one portion, but precincts with only one portion have not been subdivided, and the portion and the precinct are identical.See Clark County Metadata

name: Board of County Councilors Election Districts
description: Election Districts for Clark County Board of County Councilors; established 1/1/2015.See Clark County Metadata

name: Water Districts
description: Water service provider districts.    See Clark County Metadata

name: Traffic Impact Fee (TIF) Districts
description: Traffic Impact Fee districts are established by the traffic impact fee program to ensure that adequate transportation facilities are in place to serve development and to ensure new development pays a proportionate share of the costs of new facilities needed to serve growth.  They also ensure that developments paying fees will receive the benefit from new facilities.  TIFS only cover unincorporated Clark County and the City of Vancouver.See Clark County Metadata

name: Sewer Districts
description: Sanitary sewer district boundaries.   Defines which sewer district provides services.See Clark County Metadata

name: School Impact Fee Areas
description: School Impact Fee areas for each school district.  This layer includes the Battle Ground School District Urban Holding Sub-areas.See Clark County Metadata

name: Public Alert Grids
description: Public Alert Grids are 1/4-mile rectangles (grids) used when targeting public alert messages to specific areas in Clark County and southern Cowlitz County.   When needed, grids are selected to represent the full area that requires the message.   Using the grid codes, field commanders can easily communicate the extent of the needed alert area to CRESA and mapping (GIS) staff.  For example, a wildfire that requires an evacuation zone can be delineated in the field and the grid codes (making up the

name: Port Districts
description: Port District Boundaries and their respective Board Commissioner District Areas for Vancouver, Ridgefield, & Camas-Washougal.See Clark County Metadata

name: Metropolitan Parks District
description: The Metro Parks District is composed of the area within Vancouver's urban growth boundary and outside the city limits, per County Resolution 2004-10-02. The Metro Park District was established by a February 2005 ballot. The boundary is set as the date of the resolution and does not change due to annexations or UGA boundary adjustments.See Clark County Metadata

name: Library Taxing Districts
description: Library Taxing Districts. Coded to indicate FVRL and Camas Public Library districts.See Clark County Metadata

name: Fire District Map Pages
description: Fire District Map Pages - Map pages represent square mile rectangles covering Clark County, southern Cowlitz County and western Skamania County. They are used by fire agencies as map extents for the map books they may carry in their vehicles.  Map pages are loosely based on Public Land Survey System sections (square miles), and the unique four-digit code for each map page is based on the township, range, and section numbers.  However, they should not be used as a survey or legal description refe

name: ESD112 District Boundaries
description: Educational Service District (ESD) 112 Director Districts represent the sub-areas that each director represents.   The directors are elected by school district boards.The entire ESD112 area is represented, not just the Clark County portion.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\ESD112Districts

name: EMS Taxing Districts
description: EMS Tax Districts represent Emergency Medical Service (EMS) property tax boundaries.  See Clark County Metadata

name: Drainage Districts
description: Drainage Districts oversee funding, construction and maintenance of flood control measures such as levies and drainage systems.   They are authorized to sell bonds and levy tax assessments for funding and are managed by a board of commissioners.   Washington counties are authorized to establish drainage districts by the state legislature in 1895 and are formed by citizen petition.See Clark County Metadata

name: Cemetery Districts
description: Cemetery districtsSee Clark County Metadata

name: C-TRAN Public Transportation Benefit Areas
description: The C-Tran Public Transportation Benefit Area (PTBA) represents both the service area and tax district for the C-Tran transit agency in Clark County.See Clark County Metadata

name: Digital GIS Data Volume 2
description: Clark County GIS maintains an extensive GIS data library and updates the library on a weekly basis. The most popular GIS data that we maintain are available to download in two separate zip files.  Each zip file contains a variety of popular GIS data in Esri shapefile format.

name: Digital GIS Data Volume 1
description: Clark County GIS maintains an extensive GIS data library and updates the library on a weekly basis. The most popular GIS data that we maintain are available to download in two separate zip files.  Each zip file contains a variety of popular GIS data in Esri shapefile format.

name: Property Assessment and Taxation Data
description: Commonly requested property assessment and taxation information

name: County Boundary
description: Clark County Boundary as established in 1880.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\County

name: Surface Elevation Contours (10ft interval)
description: Surface elevation contours with 10 foot intervals created from the best available LiDAR data. The vertical datmun is North American Vertical Datum of 1988 (NAVD 88).More information about the LiDAR data flight dates and study extents can be found at https://gis.clark.wa.gov/gishome/metadata/#/layer/5190.See Clark County Metadata Data source: ..\FGDB\dw_resource.gdb\Contours10

name: Collisions
description: Collision points from State of Washington Department of Transportation.See Clark County MetadataData source: ..\FGDB\dw_Roads.gdb\Collisions

name: Growth Management Act
description: DO NOT DELETE OR MODIFY THIS ITEM. This item is managed by the ArcGIS Hub application. To make changes to this site, please visit https://hub.arcgis.com/admin/

name: Potentially Available Properties for Marijuana Facilities
description: Property boundaries for four potential marijuana facility types: Retail, Processing I, Processing II and Production.See Clark County Metadata

name: Sections
description: Public Land Survey Sections represent the boundaries of land survey features called sections, which are generally one mile by one mile rectangles and are part of Public Lands Survey System Grid. Normally, 36 sections make up a township. The section is widely used as a reference when describing the location of property and deed descriptions. See Clark County MetadataData source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\Sections

description: {{description}}

name: Clark County Surface Treatments 2023 through 2025
description: {{description}}

name: County Boundary
description: Clark County Boundary as established in 1880.See Clark County MetadataData source: ..\FGDB\dw_county.gdb\County

name: Quarter Section Maps Download
description: Clark County's official Assessor tax parcel maps to download in a bulk format.

name: Property Assessment and Treasurer Data
description:
PACS_OpenData.zip
contains most of the assessment data that people find interesting. It is a mix
of current data and certified data.  View Full Metadata
